title: narcissus tazetta var. papyraceus
intro: Narcissus tazetta var. papyraceus, also known as the paperwhite narcissus, is a type of perennial bulbous plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its white or yellow flowers and distinctive paper-like foliage. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and a laxative, due to the presence of saponins and other compounds. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta var. papyraceus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
